title: vicia sativa subsp. amphicarpa
intro: Vicia sativa subsp. amphicarpa, also known as the two-horned vetch, is a subspecies of the common vetch plant (Vicia sativa), a legume native to Europe and parts of Asia. The medicinal properties of Vicia sativa subsp. amphicarpa include its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant effects, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to relieve respiratory problems such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce menstrual cramps and alleviate symptoms of menopause. The herbal preparations of Vicia sativa subsp. amphicarpa typically involve drying and processing the plant's leaves and flowers to create teas, infusions, or tinctures that can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
